Center: OECD/NEA Nuclear Data Bank, Boulogne-Billancourt, France
ref: Jour: European Physical Journal A: Hadrons and Nuclei, Vol.60, p.120 (2024)
title: Neutron capture cross section measurement of 129I and 127I using the NaI(Tl) spectrometer of the ANNRI beamline at J-PARC

x4freetext: Neutron capture cross section measurement of 129I and
127I using the NaI(Tl) spectrometer of the ANNRI
beamline at J-PARC

x4codes.hlp: Institute of Science Tokyo, Tokyo, Japan

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: European Physical Journal A: Hadrons and Nuclei, Vol.60, p.120 (2024)

flag.hlp: Reference to related data compiled in another entry

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.A, Vol.736, p.66 (2014)

x4freetext: Energy resolution of the pulsed neutron was given.
ANNRI(BL04) beam line of MLF at J-PARC
Spallation reaction in a mercury target
irradiated with 3-GeV protons (800 kW) .
The proton beams were supplied at a repetition rate of
25 Hz and in the "double-bunch mode", in which
the pulsed protons consist of two bunches, each with
a width of 100 ns, at intervals of 600 ns.
Produced neutrons were slowed down in a supercritical
para hydrogen moderator.
Disk chopper with a semicircular shape was used to cut
the low-energy tail of neutron pulse.
The experiments were performed using the 7-mm
collimator.
The neutron spectrum was reconstructed using the 478
keV gamma-rays from gamma rays from 10B(n,alpha
gamma) 7Li reaction.
-Size: 330 mm (diam.) and 203 mm in thickness
-Angle: 90 deg with respect to the neutron beam line
-Flight length: 27.9 m
-DAQ: CAEN V1720 Digitizer (12bit, 250MHz)
127I Sample: A Na127I compressed pellet with mass of
Area density: 127I (7.18 +- 0.02 ) x10E-4 at/b
129I sample: A NaI pellet with a mass of 404 +- 1 mg
and activity of 2.65 MBq, shielded in a Ti case.
The sample contained 129I with an isotopic
abundance of 85.9%, together with 14.1% of 127I as an
impurity.
Area density: 127I (0.99 +- 0.01) x10E-4 at/b
Compiled by A.Kimura
Measurements with the carbon sample and
"Blank (no sample) " were used for background
estimation. The weighting function was normalized by
comparing the experimental results of the Au sample
at the 1st resonance using the saturated resonance
method.
The dead-time correction was applied with the
extended dead-time model. Correction factors for
self-shielding and multiple scattering effects were
deduced using the Monte-Carlo simulation code PHITS.
Total uncertainty
Statistical uncertainty
Unc. due to neutron flux
Unc. due to deadtime correction
Unc. due to frame-overlap subtraction
Unc. due to self-shielding and multiple
scattering correction
Unc. due to area density of 127I
Unc. due to normalization
Unc. due to weighting function calculation
Unc. due to PHWT
Data were obtained from AUTHOR.
Fig. 12 in the REFERENCE.

Value of quantity specified under REACTION

x4freetext: Resonance parameters were extracted from a
fit of the capture cross-section using the REFIT.
- The ANNRI beamline resolution function was taken
from the work of Kino. See REL-REF.
- The initial resonance parametrization data,
the resonance energy, radiative width, neutron
width, and the spin and parity for each resonance
were taken from JENDL-5.
- The fitting regions were up to 100eV.
- In the energy range over 50 eV, only values for
the neutron width were determined.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainty of resonance energy was
deduced from the uncertainty of the fitting result by
REFIT, the flight length, and the initial time delay.
The other uncertainties are the ones provided by REFIT.
Table 5 in the REFERENCE.
Capture cross section.

x4freetext: The thermal neutron capture cross section was deduced
from the resonance parameters in 23615.004.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainty of the thermal capture
cross section was derived from the variation between
the measured cross section and the fitting result by
REFIT in the neutron energy range from 0.01 to 0.1 eV.
Table 7 in the REFERENCE

x4freetext: Measurements with the carbon sample,
"Blank (no sample) ", and "Dummy case with Na127I"
were used for background estimation.
The sample contained 14.1% of 127I as an impurity.
The neutron capture cross section for 129I was
normalized to the results of 127I in 23615003,
at four large resonances of 127I, with considering
the isotopic abundance of 127I.
The influence of 127I in the sample was not subtracted.
The presented data is sum of capture cross section of
The dead-time correction was applied with the
extended dead-time model.
Correction factors for self-shielding and multiple
scattering effects for the 129 sample and the dummy
case were deduced using the Monte-Carlo simulation
code PHITS.
Total uncertainty
Statistical uncertainty
Unc. due to neutron flux
Unc. due to deadtime correction
Unc. due to frame-overlap subtraction
Unc. due to self-shielding and multiple
scattering correction for the dummy case.
Unc. due to self-shielding and multiple
scattering correction for the 129I sample.
Unc. due to area density of 129I
Unc. due to isotopic abundance
Unc. due to normalization
Unc. due to weighting function calculation
Unc. due to PHWT
Unc. due to capture cross section of 127I
Data were obtained from AUTHOR.

Value of quantity specified under REACTION

x4freetext: Resonance parameters were extracted from a
fit of the capture cross-section using the REFIT.
- The ANNRI beamline resolution function was taken
from the work of Kino. See REL-REF.
- The initial resonance parametrization data,
the resonance energy, radiative width, neutron
width, and the spin and parity for each resonance
were taken from JENDL-5.
- The values for the gamma width were fixed, and
the values for the resonance energy the neutron
width were determined.
- The gamma width for a negative resonance is fixed as
106 meV, which is the averaged value in JENDL-5.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainty of resonance energy was
deduced from the uncertainty of the fitting result by
REFIT, the flight length, and the initial time delay.
The other uncertainties are the ones provided by REFIT.
Table 6 in the REFERENCE.
At the request of the author, the spin J of the 41.37-
and 96.44-eV resonances were revised from 2 to 4.
The revised values are those listed in JENDL-5.
Capture cross section.
